ddls:
CREATE TABLE "weather_data" (
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather record */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the data was recorded */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the location where the data was collected */,
  "statusN" REAL /* Status code for the weather condition (N) */,
  "statusS" REAL /* Status code for the weather condition (S) */,
  "tempC" REAL /* Temperature in degrees Celsius */,
  "windspeedMiles" REAL /* Wind speed in miles per hour */,
  "precipMM" REAL /* Precipitation in millimeters */,
  "humidity" REAL /* Humidity percentage */,
  "visibility" REAL /* Visibility in kilometers */,
  "pressure" REAL /* Air pressure in millibars */,
  "cloudcover" REAL /* Cloud cover percentage */,
  "heatIndexC" REAL /* Heat index in degrees Celsius */,
  "dewPointC" REAL /* Dew point in degrees Celsius */,
  "windChillC" REAL /* Wind chill in degrees Celsius */,
  "windGustMiles" REAL /* Wind gust speed in miles per hour */,
  "feelsLikeC" REAL /* Feels like temperature in degrees Celsius */,
  "elevation" TEXT /* Elevation of the location where the data was collected */,
  "weather_station_id" TEXT /* Identifier for the specific weather station that collected the data */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the data (e.g., satellite, ground station, API) */,
  "data_quality" TEXT /* Measure of the quality of the data (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the data was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("record_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_statusN FOREIGN KEY ("statusN") REFERENCES status_codes ("status_code_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_statusS FOREIGN KEY ("statusS") REFERENCES status_codes ("status_code_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude coordinate of the location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude coordinate of the location */,
  "elevation" REAL /* Elevation of the location */,
  "timezone" TEXT /* Timezone of the location */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the location is situated */,
  "state_or_province" TEXT /* State or province where the location is situated */,
  "city" TEXT /* City where the location is situated */,
  "description" TEXT /* Additional details about the location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "status_codes" (
  "status_code_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status code */,
  "status_code" TEXT /* Status code (e.g., statusN, statusS) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the weather condition represented by the status code */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the weather condition (e.g., temperature, precipitation, wind) */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the weather condition (e.g., low, moderate, high, critical) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_code_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Contact number of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department within the organization the user belongs to */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the location for which the alert is set */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who set the alert */,
  "condition" TEXT /* Condition that triggers the alert (e.g., temperature, wind speed) */,
  "threshold" REAL /* Threshold value for the condition */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Message to be sent when the alert is triggered */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., email, SMS, in-app notification) */,
  "alert_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency at which the alert should be triggered (e.g., immediate, daily, weekly) */,
  "alert_status" TEXT /* Status of the alert (e.g., active, disabled, triggered) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who created the report */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the report */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the report */,
  "data_range" TEXT /* Data range covered by the report */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the report was created */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., temperature, wind, precipitation) */,
  "report_format" TEXT /* Format of the report (e.g., PDF, CSV, HTML) */,
  "report_status" TEXT /* Status of the report (e.g., draft, published, archived) */,
  "last_modified" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the report was last modified */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user accessing the data */,
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the weather record being accessed */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the data was accessed */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used to access the data */,
  "data_size" INTEGER /* Size of the data accessed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_access_record_id FOREIGN KEY ("record_id") REFERENCES weather_data ("record_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "collaborations" (
  "collaboration_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each collaboration */,
  "initiator_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who initiated the collaboration */,
  "collaborator_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who is collaborating */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the collaboration */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the collaboration */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the collaboration (e.g., active, completed) */,
  "project_name" TEXT /* Name of the project for which the collaboration is being conducted */,
  "project_description" TEXT /* Description of the project */,
  "collaboration_type" TEXT /* Type of collaboration (e.g., research, analysis, reporting) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("collaboration_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_collaborations_initiator_id FOREIGN KEY ("initiator_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_collaborations_collaborator_id FOREIGN KEY ("collaborator_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sharing" (
  "sharing_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data sharing event */,
  "data_owner_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who owns the data */,
  "shared_with_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user with whom the data is shared */,
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the weather record being shared */,
  "sharing_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the data was shared */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions granted for the shared data */,
  "sharing_reason" TEXT /* Reason for sharing the data */,
  "shared_data_size" INTEGER /* Size of the data being shared */,
  "sharing_status" TEXT /* Status of the sharing event (e.g., pending, approved, rejected) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sharing_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_sharing_shared_with_id FOREIGN KEY ("shared_with_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_sharing_data_owner_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_owner_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_sharing_record_id FOREIGN KEY ("record_id") REFERENCES weather_data ("record_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather metric */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the metric (e.g., temperature, wind speed) */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the metric */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the metric */,
  "metric_type" TEXT /* Type of metric (e.g., temperature, wind, precipitation) */,
  "metric_category" TEXT /* Category of the metric (e.g., atmospheric, ground, satellite) */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the metric data (e.g., sensor, API, model) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id")
)
